title: Orana Wildlife Park Review
meta_desc: Discover NZ's only open-range zoo, Orana Wildlife Park in Christchurch. Ride through African Savannah, hand-feed giraffes, and explore native wildlife. Perfect for families, read our honest review for tips and savings.
intro: Orana Wildlife Park in Christchurch is a must-visit attraction for families. As NZ's only open-range zoo, it offers a unique and exciting experience for kids of all ages. With its African Savannah enclosure and native wildlife section, there's something for everyone to enjoy.

sections.heading: What to Expect
sections.body:
Orana Wildlife Park is an amazing place to visit with kids. The open-range zoo allows animals to roam freely, giving you a chance to get up close and personal with them. The African Savannah enclosure is a highlight, where you can ride through by bus while lions, giraffes, and rhinos roam alongside. Don't miss the daily giraffe feeding at 2pm, where kids can hand-feed these gentle giants.

The native wildlife section is also excellent, with a variety of birds, reptiles, and mammals to see. With a minimum age of 0+, this attraction is suitable for all ages, making it perfect for families with young children.
list: African Savannah enclosure with lions, giraffes, and rhinos
Native wildlife section with birds, reptiles, and mammals

sections.heading: Value for Money and Insider Tips
sections.body:
With prices starting from $14 per child and $32 per adult, Orana Wildlife Park offers great value for money. The park is open from 10am to 5pm, and we recommend allowing at least 3-4 hours to explore. To make the most of your visit, consider arriving early to beat the crowds and make the most of the animal encounters.

For insider tips, be sure to check the park's website for special deals and discounts. You can also book your tickets online in advance to save time and money. Additionally, consider packing a picnic lunch to enjoy in the park's designated areas.

sections.heading: Best Time to Visit and Booking
sections.body:
The best time to visit Orana Wildlife Park is during the summer months when the weather is warm and sunny. However, be aware that this is also the peak tourist season, so expect larger crowds. If you're looking for a more relaxed experience, consider visiting during the shoulder season.

To book your tickets, simply visit the Orana Wildlife Park website and follow the online booking process. You can also purchase tickets at the gate, but be aware that prices may be higher. For families, consider purchasing a family pass, which can offer significant savings.
list: Book tickets online in advance to save time and money
Consider purchasing a family pass for savings
Check the park's website for special deals and discounts

sections.heading: Downsides for Families
sections.body:
While Orana Wildlife Park is an excellent attraction for families, there are a few downsides to be aware of. The park can get quite crowded during peak season, which may be overwhelming for young children. Additionally, the bus ride through the African Savannah enclosure can be a bit bumpy, so be sure to hold on to your little ones.

Despite these minor downsides, Orana Wildlife Park is an excellent choice for families. With its unique animal encounters, native wildlife section, and daily giraffe feeding, there's something for everyone to enjoy. Just be sure to plan ahead, book your tickets in advance, and arrive early to make the most of your visit.
